entries.zone_name: Ascent of the Awakened: Audience with the Guardian
entries.polished_md:
## Mechanics

The primary danger posed by this Dragon is **Breath of Fire**, which spawns 2 flame blobs for every player or pet within the Area of Effect! This will instantly overwhelm a raid!

## Positioning & Pull

Place everyone well out of AE range, pull the Dragon and the tank must get him nose into a crook near an alcove. Now it is a long slow burn.

- **MT's healers** must joust in between the **Breath of Flame** attacks to heal, then get OUT of AE range before the **50 seconds** expires.
- As soon as the 2 mobs (for the MT) spawn, **ST** runs in, grabs them, and drags them well out of range and, assisted by all the melee'rs, kills them.
- **DPS** can assist a little, but all the long-range DPS (**mages** and **bowmen**) should be on the Dragon.
- Use all hate reducers to make sure the **MT** does not lose agro or it is all over!

## Notes

Take your time! Use your hearts and shards! Play the waiting game and you will win.
- Speak with this NPC to spawn [K'Dal the Deceiver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/K'Dal_the_Deceiver).

- Will teleport plater underwater. Without a way to breathe underwater the player will drown.
- Joust his stifle effect or cure

## Trash Clear

Clear all drakes on the lower platform in a (clockwise or counterclockwise), leaving the droags for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time. **KILL FAST**, aiming for clearing in **10-15 minutes**!

> If you're not averaging **13k DPS** or better don't bother do this.

Move raid to top of stairs and body pull the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar.

## Pull

Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull Harla Dar to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of **knockback**.

entries.zone_name: Drayek's Chamber: Throne of the Kromise King
entries.polished_md:
## Positioning

It is recommended that you pull Darathar into the water, and have casters positioned at maximum range to avoid incurable trauma damage.

## Self-Heals & Stuns

He will cast a raid-wide **stun** and heals for **25% of his maximum health** three times during the fight, at **75%**, **50%** and **25%** health. This means:

- At **75%** health, he will heal to full health.
- At **50%** he heals to **75%** health.
- At **25%** he heals back to **50%** health.

After that point, he will no longer heal himself.

## Flame of Ages

His special attack, [Flame of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_of_Ages), is an **elemental area-of-effect** spell which does a high amount of direct damage and has a damage-over-time component. He casts this approximately every **50 seconds**.

> Each member should have **elemental cure potions** to ensure they can cure themselves in case a healer is unable to cure them immediately.

## Per-Instance Heal Behaviour

The self-heals only occur **once per instance** at each health threshold, not per encounter. For example, if you get Darathar below **50%** and then the raid wipes, Darathar will only heal once during subsequent fights. If the raid wipes after taking Darathar to **25%** health, he will not heal and stun again in that instance.

## Further Reading

See the [Talk:Darathar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talk:Darathar) page for more strategies and suggestions for defeating Darathar.
- Talk to him to start the encounter.

- Will summon every brownie trickster and prankster in the zone, Kill them first then Hes just a tank and spank.
- Be sure to also kill the Bixies and the Far Strider Unicorn if its up, they will all assist him in the battle if not killed first.

## Overview

Sarik is an **endurance fight**. The idea of the fight is to outlast the waves of **level 74 ^^^** [fang pack members](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_fang_pack_member) that Sarik will spawn periodically.

## Strategy

- Use your **AOE DPS** to take out the wolves while the **main tank** fights Sarik in a save position.
- When the raid DPS kills the adds they should focus back on Sarik.
- Our adds totaled to another **4,000,000 HP** alone.

Repeat this process until the boss is defeated.

- Tunare is the deity of most [Fae](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Fae).
- Tunare appears in-game as an older humanoid female wearing flowing robes and a crown of vines and leaves.

- Heals from 1% to 25%
- Adds can be mesmerized (as of Jan 2007)
- Nukes hard on 2%

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:
- [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) - Healer
- [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) - Mage
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) - Fighters
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) - Scout

## Positioning

- Position **MT** in one corner nearest the door to his room.
- Raid in other corner near door.
- **MT** pulls, **OT** pulls adds to corner opposite MT.
- **Healers** stay in corner near door, away from MT.
- All **casters** go to opposite corner and stay there.

## Kill Order

Burn adds, then name. **Melee** jousts **Spinning Fury** (**50s** timer). Mob is quite easy.

entries.zone_name: The Unkempt Vale: A Lordly Confrontation
entries.polished_md: - If you write 'Qeynos' backwards you get 'Sonyeq', or [Sony](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Daybreak_Game_Company,_LLC) [EQ](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/EverQuest)! ''(Source - [Chillispike](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/User:Chillispike))

- If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

## Anecdote

I recently duo'd Trakanon with a guildie's Mystic and our two mercs. We didn't have a Chelsith stone or anything, but still managed to kill him. It was a tough fight though. He's definitely much more doable now in COE end game gear. Gonna' get me one of those cool looking dragon head shields sooner or later!

## Zone Layout

- When you enter you are in a zone in/zone out room which leads to gigantic stairs. You must use the side area of the stairs to walk up into the main lair.
- When you first enter the zone, trakanon will greet you from far above the platform, then fly down. At this point you can engage trakanon.

## Chelsith Stone & The Orb

- You must have a **chelsith stone** with you to have any chance to succeed. It will drop in the leviathan encounter in-game. *(Obsolete as of end-game CoE, heroic ToV, If you have a tank with you, otherwise you will probably still need an orb.)*
- Once the fight begins, you will notice the holder of a chelsith stone can touch the **orb altar** on the far side of the platform to energize it. Once energized if you stay within **14m** of the energized orb structure, you are protected from many of trakanons more powerful abilities.
- The key to winning this encounter is to realize you must all fight within **14m** of the activated orb on the far side of the platform. Trakanons **arcane maelstrom** breath weapon is far too lethal outside this protective area. In addition trakanons knockback abilities will prove problematic, and all but one of them is negated when you are close to this orb object (if its energized)

## Mechanics

### Wing Buffet & Tail Swipe

Trakanon is a **level 87 warlock**. He will periodically cast **Wing Buffet** and **Tail Swipe**.

- **Wing Buffet** will **STUN** everyone in front of him for **2.5 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK** everyone back UNLESS they are within **14m** of the energized orb.
- **Tail Swipe** does some damage, and stuns players for about **3 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK BACK** any players that are NOT within **14m** of the energized orb.

### Immunities & Attack Profile

Trakanon is immune to all control spells, and will double attack on melee **10%** more than normal.

### Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord / Pestilential Pause

Trakanon has **Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord** on him. On take damage **10%** of the time, players will be hit with **Pestilential Pause**. This does a slight amount of damage to the player and for the next **10 seconds**, all the players spells take much longer to cast, and are basically at half effectiveness (heals and damage are half as effective as normal). Also your attack speed is reduced considerably during this time. This effect is **not dispellable**. Also, **2%** of the time trakanon is hit with a hostile spell, he will heal himself for **2%**.

### Prophetic Scrutiny

Trakanon will keep **PROPHETIC SCRUTINY** on one player at all times. With this effect on you, any spells you cast are interrupted, if the spell is interruptable. Every time you start to cast a spell you will be hit with **PROPHETIC BOLT**, which a small amount of damage and interrupts the spell you are starting to cast. This should be active on one player in the raid at all times

### Prescient Gaze

Trakanon will keep **Prescient gaze** on all players at all times. It should never be off you for more than a few seconds even if you die and are rezzed back in. It causes all players to mana burn, using health whenever they use mana. It also causes players to have **prescient intervention** cast on them **8%** of the time they try and cast a beneficial spell. Prescient intervention interrupts the spell they are trying to cast, and stifles the player for **2 seconds**, and does a small amount of damage to the players mana pool.

### Mark of Repulsion / Static Repulsion

One player will generally have **MARK OF REPULSION** on him/her. This spell will go off at some random time, never less than **30 secs** after it was placed on you. It has an hourglass icon.

> When it goes off that player will be hit with **static repulsion**, a targeted AOE that WILL knock back all players within **22m** of the person who had Mark of Repulsion on him/her. You are **NEVER** protected from this knockback, even if near the orb. You must move away from your raid party so as not to endanger them with your Mark of Repulsion/Static Repulsion combo.

### Arcane Maelstrom

**3.5 seconds AFTER** static repulsion goes off, Trakanon will cast **Arcane Maelstrom**. A powerful aoe that does heat damage, does damage to a random item in everyones inventory, and does massive **MAGIC BASED** health and mana damage UNLESS you are close to the energized orb. You avoid MAGIC BASED MANA AND HEALTH DAMAGE only if you are near the energized orb.

### Fetid Cloud & Toxic Tempest

Trakanon will periodically cast **Fetid Cloud** and **Toxic Tempest** as well.

- **Fetid Cloud** does disease based health damage the closer you are to trak the more disease damage you take, and slows your attack speed. If you have **Toxic Tempest** still on you when fetid cloud hits you, you will take **ADDITIONAL MASSIVE poison based health damage**.
- **Toxic Tempest** works similarly to Fetid Cloud, toxic tempest does range relative, poison based health damage to all players. If you have **fetid cloud** on you when Toxic tempest hits you, you will take additional **MASSIVE disease damage** as well. Toxic tempest slows your movement rate as well.

## Enrage / Soft Timer

**800 seconds** after the raid fight begins, the platform you fight on begins to lower into the lava. This takes about **20-30 secs**, after that time you will be forced to fight trakanon in the lava, and it won't likely work well for you.

- If you hop away from the platform, you wont be protected by the orb so that's not really an option.
- If you stay near the orb, you will be covered in lava so that's not really an option.

You have only about **820 secs** to win this fight.

If trakanon dies, the platform will rise. If trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

## Pull & Positioning

**MT** can pull this named out of the room, which is recommended. Pull him up the ramp and have the **MT** park against one of the pillars in the upper hallway. He has a pretty hefty AoE on a **1:35 second** timer. (seen **55sec - 1min 45sec**) Stand about **50 meters** away and you should be good. (End of the ramp around the corner seems good.)

Silverwing may need to be pulled into the hallway because of a new **healing-to-100%** effect which was added in **Gu53**.

## Warding Statues (80%)

***Beginning when Silverwing's health hits 80%***, someone in the raid will be ultra-visioned. It will be announced zonewide: "(target)is now seeing through their mind's eye!"

That person will need to go deactivate the large warding statue by clicking the small device *on the floor next to the large statue*. It can be difficult to see on many computers, so be sure to hover over the floor all around the warding statues.

> Until that person deactivates the statue, Silverwing will be ***immune to damage***, but he ***will*** continue to AoE.

### Statue Assignments

- **Fighters** deactivate the first statue on the left when entering Silverwing's room. (SouthWest)
- **Scouts** deactivate the center far side opposite Silverwing. (East)
- **Mages** deactivate the statue opposite the fighters. (Northwest)
- **Priests** deactivate the one where Silverwing resides before he is pulled. (West)

**Note:** If you have not reprogrammed the wards already you will ***not*** be able to deactivate them now.

## Adds (60%)

***Starting at 60% of Silverwing's health,*** adds will start to spawn. A successful strategy is to mesmerize these until Silverwing is Immune, then focus on the adds while the ultra-visioned person works on deactivating their ward statue.

## Levers

Also, there are two **levers** flanking Silverwing's original position. Deactivating one will summon a droag to turn it back on. Put two people on those levers and you will have less problems with the **stuns**.

## Overview

This is really a **burn fight**.

## Sacs (every ~30s)

Tairiza spawn **"sacs"** approximately every **30 seconds**. These sac will land on a random person in the raid which will incapacitate them as well as kill them over time.

- Have **ranged DPS** and **healers** to the left or right of the door with a **Ranged DPS main assist** calling the sacs and making the ranged dps cycle between killing the sacs then immediately back on the named.
- **Melee DPS** needs to stand right under the big butt of the Widow Mistress and target the mob directly and **BURN IT DOWN**.
- If a sac spawns on **melee dps** or the **Main Tank** it's easy for the Melee dps to just TAB, burn the sac down, then resume beating the spider.

## Add Phase (40%)

At approximately **40%** she spawns a group of spiders. Once this happens pretty much ignore the sacs and focus the burn on the mob. A **zerker** or strong AE'ing **offtank** needs to try to get as many spiders beating on them as possible.

> **NOTE:** The spider adds are immune to all taunts. Another option if you have multiple **Enchanters** is to mezz them. We still do it that way. Either way you want to burn Tairiza as fast as possible once the adds start. If you have big spells/CAs with long recast timers, make sure you save them for that final push.

## Final Burn

Once the spiders pop everyone in the raid target the named directly and just **BURN BURN BURN**. Some people will die. They need to revive and get back in. AE's will burn a lot of the spiders down, but prolly not all of them so once you kill the named kill the remaining spiders and loot the **metal chest**.
